name: Cheesy Polenta with Tomato & Basil Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, plus extra for greasing
salt and freshly ground black pepper
4 tablespoons chopped fresh herbs (such as oregano, chives, and flat-leaf parsley)
500 grams canned creamed tomatoes or passata
3 tablespoons chopped fresh basil, plus extra for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a 10 x 6 ½ inch dish with vegetable oil. In a large pan, bring 2 liters (approximately 8 pints) of water to a boil. Add the polenta and whisk constantly. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring frequently for 10-15 minutes or until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan.
2. Stir in the herbs, Parmesan cheese, salt, and pepper. Transfer to the prepared dish and let cool.
3. To make the Tomato & Basil sauce, crush the garlic and heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. Fry the garlic for 30 seconds (do not brown). Add the creamed tomatoes, bay leaf, thyme, and a generous pinch of sugar. Season with salt and pepper, then bring to a boil and simmer uncovered for 5-10 minutes. Remove the bay leaf and thyme sprig, then add the chopped basil.
4. To serve, cut the polenta into pieces and lightly brush with oil. Preheat a griddle pan or grill and fry each side for 3-4 minutes, or grill under a preheated grill for 7-8 minutes on each side. Serve with Tomato & Basil sauce, fresh Parmesan shavings, and chopped basil leaves.
To prepare ahead: Complete the recipe up to step 3. Cover and refrigerate separately for up to two days. To serve: Complete the recipe as directed.
